hypothesis: a global skill with repo-local trackers is the highest-leverage default for uncertain execution work
action: designed the skill as a global package and targeted docs/execution/<task-slug>/ inside the active repo
expected_signal: the skill remains reusable across repos while each task stays inspectable near the codebase
result: the design stays cross-repo and the sample tracker folder was created successfully in this repo
decision: keep the global-skill plus repo-local tracker default
playbook_note: separate the reusable skill from repo-local execution memory

hypothesis: a separate theory file is redundant when experiments already capture the hypothesis and result
action: collapsed the tracker design into task.md, experiments.jsonl, and playbook.md only
expected_signal: the tracker becomes easier for both humans and llms to maintain without drift between duplicate files
result: the implemented skill has no theory file and uses task.md as the current synthesized view
decision: keep theories inside task.md instead of a separate file
playbook_note: keep experiments as truth and task.md as synthesis

hypothesis: two tiny helper scripts are enough to keep the adaptive loop disciplined without adding heavy infrastructure
action: implemented init_task.py and append_experiment.py and exercised both on this repo
expected_signal: the workflow can create a task folder, append structured experiments, and preserve existing logs
result: dry-run, real init, and experiment append all completed successfully
decision: ship the lightweight script pair as the default operational surface
playbook_note: prefer small deterministic helpers over heavy tracker machinery

hypothesis: the initialized skill package and tracker workflow are stable enough to be reused immediately
action: validated the skill package, regenerated agents/openai.yaml, and reran init_task.py against the sample task folder
expected_signal: validation passes and rerunning task initialization preserves the existing experiments log
result: quick validation passed, metadata regenerated cleanly, and rerunning init_task.py preserved the three existing experiment entries
decision: mark the sample workflow as complete and reusable
evidence: init_task rerun left experiments.jsonl line count unchanged at 3 before the new append
playbook_note: verify idempotence before trusting tracker automation
